title: Social Fermentation
definition: The slow, underground process of change in society that suddenly surfaces in visible, tangible ways.

proofs.description: Extracted from conversation via CADMIES Harvest Pipeline
proofs.reference: CADMIES Harvest Pipeline v4.0 — raw dump rescue

difficulty_levels.beginner: Social change often happens invisibly before it becomes obvious.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The slow, underground process of change in society that suddenly surfaces in visible, tangible ways.
difficulty_levels.expert: Social fermentation models societal transformation as analogous to biological fermentation: invisible preparatory processes occurring beneath the surface of public awareness that create the conditions for sudden, visible emergence of new social structures.

extra_fields.insight: One's actions can create new possibilities and inspire others to follow suit, thereby creating positive social transformation.
extra_fields.historical_context: The concept of social fermentation emerged as a metaphor to describe the process of societal transformation.
limitations: May oversimplify the complex nature of societal change
Assumes that societal change always follows a gradual process
applications: Designing strategies for gradual social change
